skill.name: context-compression
skill.description: Guides AI agents through structured context compression, token-use reduction, and quality evaluation for long-running sessions.
skill.summary: Compress long agent sessions while preserving decisions, file history, and next steps.
skill.author: ChakshuGautam

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged external commands, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and sensitive data patterns. Review found these are false positives from markdown code fences, evaluation examples, and ordinary terms, with no evidence of malicious intent. The skill includes a Python evaluation helper, so the final risk is low rather than safe.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static command execution alerts are false positives. The cited locations are markdown examples, prompt templates, or a non-executed Python snippet inside documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced examples or docstrings, not executable shell calls. The Python file does not import subprocess or invoke a shell.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match context-compression terminology and evaluation wording. No cryptographic library use or weak hash implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain no crypto imports, digest functions, or password handling. The matches are semantic collisions with compression and evaluation language.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Evaluation Questions
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts are false positives from example probe questions about files, commands, and session state. They do not collect host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text discusses evaluating whether compressed context remembers prior file operations. No system commands, environment enumeration, or host scanning behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Runnable Evaluation Helper Requires Normal Script Review
low_findings.description: The package includes a Python helper for probe generation and compression evaluation. It uses local regex parsing and a caller-provided response function, with no built-in network or filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The helper is executable code, so it should remain visible as a low risk factor. Review found only standard library imports and no direct exfiltration or command execution behavior.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions lose important details when context windows fill. This skill provides structured compression methods that preserve intent, file changes, decisions, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains anchored iterative summarization for long agent sessions.
Defines summary sections for intent, files, decisions, state, and next steps.
Compares anchored, opaque, and regenerative compression methods.
Provides probe types for testing compressed context quality.
Includes a Python helper for local compression evaluation experiments.
Recommends tokens-per-task as the main optimization metric.
limitations: Does not provide a production LLM judge integration.
Uses simplified token and fact extraction heuristics in the helper script.
Does not guarantee perfect preservation of file history.
Requires users to adapt templates to their agent workflow.

use_cases.title: Preserve coding session history
use_cases.description: Create structured summaries that keep modified files, current failures, decisions, and remaining work visible after compaction.

use_cases.title: Design compression evaluations
use_cases.description: Build recall, artifact, continuation, and decision probes to test whether compressed context remains useful.

use_cases.title: Reduce token waste
use_cases.description: Optimize for total tokens per completed task instead of only reducing tokens per request.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a session
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use after a long conversation
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress this conversation into sections for session intent, files modified, decisions made, current state, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Preserve file history
prompt_templates.scenario: Coding session with many file operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a context compression summary that separates files modified, files read, test status, errors, and remaining actions.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate compression quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing whether a summary preserved important details
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate recall, artifact, continuation, and decision probes for this compressed context. Include expected answers for each probe.

prompt_templates.title: Design a compression system
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow design for agent infrastructure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an anchored iterative summarization workflow for our agent. Include trigger thresholds, merge rules, evaluation probes, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Compress a debugging session after many turns.
output_examples.output: A structured summary with session intent, root cause, files changed, test status, and ordered next steps.

output_examples.input: Test whether a compressed summary retained key details.
output_examples.output: A set of targeted probe questions covering original errors, changed files, decisions, and pending work.

output_examples.input: Choose a compression strategy for a long coding agent run.
output_examples.output: A comparison of anchored, opaque, and regenerative approaches with a recommendation based on re-fetching cost.

best_practices: Optimize for total tokens per completed task.
Use fixed summary sections for file history and decisions.
Test summaries with probes before relying on them.
anti_patterns: Compressing aggressively without checking lost details.
Regenerating full summaries when incremental merging is enough.
Tracking files only in free-form prose.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps agents keep useful context when long sessions exceed practical context limits.

faq.question: Does it run compression automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides methods, templates, and a local evaluation helper.

faq.question: Which compression method does it prefer?
faq.answer: It favors anchored iterative summarization when file tracking and continuity matter.

faq.question: Can it evaluate compressed context?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes probe-based evaluation and includes a script with heuristic scoring.

faq.question: Is it only for coding tasks?
faq.answer: No. The concepts can apply elsewhere, but the examples focus on coding agents.

faq.question: Does it include external API calls?
faq.answer: No direct API call is implemented. The script includes a documented placeholder for production judge integration.
